html:
[5655]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 缚妇民 - Unclear term, possibly referring to a specific social or cultural practice involving the capture or control of women.
  • 率少年持白梃 - Literally 'lead young men with white sticks,' suggesting an organized group of young men armed with sticks, possibly for a specific purpose.
  • 俟过即共擒缚 - Literally 'wait until they pass and then capture and bind,' indicating a planned ambush or capture.
  • 首罪 - Literally 'admit guilt,' but in context, it suggests a ritual or formal acknowledgment of wrongdoing.
  • 俗谓之妇也 - Customarily called '妇,' where '妇' is an unclear term that may need further research.
  • 南海人 - Refers to people from the Nanhai region, historically corresponding to parts of modern-day Guangdong and Guangxi provinces.
  • 每沐以灰投流水中 - Literally 'each time they bathe by throwing ashes into the flowing water,' describing a specific bathing ritual.
  • 就水以沐以彘膏其髪 - Literally 'wash their hair in the water and grease it with pig fat,' describing a hair care practice.
  • 髠鬻于市 - Literally 'shave and sell in the market,' indicating a practice of selling shaved heads.
  • 刺其胸前作花 - Literally 'pierce their chests to make flowers,' describing a body modification practice.
  • 有物如粉而紫色 - Literally 'there is something like powder and purple,' describing a material or substance.
  • 画其两目下去前二齿 - Literally 'draw their two eyes and remove the two front teeth,' describing a form of body modification or ritual.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南海异事 - Refers to 'Strange Events in Nanhai,' suggesting a collection of local legends or historical accounts from the Nanhai region.
  • 日南 - Refers to the southernmost region of ancient China, possibly corresponding to modern-day Vietnam or other areas south of the Yangtze River.
  • 天寳实録 - Refers to the 'Tianbao Record,' a historical text from the Tang Dynasty, possibly providing additional context or verification for the events described.

Proper Nouns

  • 缚妇民 - An unclear term that may refer to a specific social or cultural practice.
  • 南海 - The Nanhai region, historically corresponding to parts of modern-day Guangdong and Guangxi provinces.
  • 日南 - The southernmost region of ancient China, possibly corresponding to modern-day Vietnam or other areas south of the Yangtze River.
  • 天寳实録 - A historical text from the Tang Dynasty, possibly providing additional context or verification for the events described.

[5656]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 贡却火雀一雌一雄 - Mythical bird that repels fire, described as one male and one female.
  • 履水珠 - Mythical pearl that allows the wearer to walk on water.
  • 常坚冰 - Type of ice that never melts.
  • 变画草 - Plant that can change its appearance or drawing.
  • 其声清亮不并寻常禽鸟 - Clear and bright sound, unlike ordinary birds.
  • 置于烈火中而火自散 - Placed in intense fire, the fire disperses on its own.
  • 上嘉其异 - Emperor admires its uniqueness.
  • 遂盛于火精笼悬于寝殿 - Then placed in a fire spirit cage and hung in the bedroom.
  • 夜则宫人并蜡炬烧之 - At night, palace ladies lit wax candles.
  • 终不能损其毛羽 - Could not damage its feathers.
  • 其上鳞皴其中有窍 - Scaly and wrinkled, with a hole inside.
  • 云将入江海可长行洪波之上下 - Said to enter rivers and seas, can travel through surging waves.
  • 上始不谓之实 - Emperor initially did not believe it was true.
  • 遂命善防者以五色丝贯之系之于左臂 - Then ordered skilled protectors to use multi-colored silk to tie it to the left arm.
  • 毒龙畏之 - Venomous dragon feared it.
  • 遣入龙池 - Sent into the dragon pool.
  • 其人则步骤于波上若在平地 - Walked on the waves as if on flat ground.
  • 因以御馔赐使人 - Then gave imperial food to the envoy.
  • 至长庆中嫔御试弄于海池上 - By the time of the Longqing era, ladies-in-waiting tried it on the sea pool.
  • 遂化为异龙入于池内 - Then transformed into a strange dragon and entered the pool.
  • 俄而云烟暴起不复追讨矣 - Soon, clouds and smoke suddenly arose, and it was no longer pursued.
  • 常坚冰云其国有大凝山 - Ever-frozen ice, said to be from a great freezing mountain in the kingdom.
  • 其中有氷千年不释 - Ice that does not melt for a thousand years.
  • 及赍至京师洁冷如故 - When brought to the capital, it remained as clean and cold as before.
  • 虽盛暑赫日终不消嚼之 - Even in the height of summer, it would not melt even if eaten.
  • 与中国氷冻无异 - Compared to China, there is no difference in freezing.
  • 变昼草芭蕉可长数尺而一茎千叶 - Day-changing grass, like a banana plant, can grow several feet tall with a thousand leaves on one stem.
  • 树之则百步内昏黒如夜 - Plant it, and within a hundred steps, it becomes as dark as night.
  • 及上见而怒曰背明向暗此草何足贵也 - When the emperor saw it and became angry, saying it turned away from light and towards darkness, what is this grass worth?
  • 命并匣焚之于使前 - Ordered to burn it along with the box in front of the envoy.
  • 使初不为乐及退谓鸿胪曰本国以变昼为异今皇帝以向暗为非可谓明德矣 - The envoy did not find it enjoyable, and after leaving, he said to the Honglu, in our country, changing the day is considered extraordinary, but the emperor considers turning towards darkness as wrong, which shows his enlightened virtue.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 顺宗即位年 - Year 805 AD when Emperor Shunzong ascended the throne.
  • 拘弥国 - Historical kingdom located in present-day Xinjiang, China.
  • 贡却火雀 - Mythical bird known for its ability to repel fire.
  • 履水珠 - Mythical pearl that allows the wearer to walk on water.
  • 常坚冰 - Symbolizes purity and endurance.
  • 变画草 - Symbolizes transformation and adaptability.
  • 长庆中 - Refers to the Longqing era, a period during the Tang Dynasty (821-824 AD).

Proper Nouns

  • 顺宗 - Emperor Shunzong.
  • 长庆 - Era name of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 鸿胪 - Title of a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty.

[5657]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 星囘節 - Traditional festival marking the winter solstice, symbolizing the cyclical nature of time.
  • 避風台 - Platform used for shelter during harsh weather, often associated with ceremonial or leisure activities.
  • 震旦 - Ancient name for China, used in historical and literary contexts.
  • 翊衛 - Assist in protection or guard, often used in the context of royal or imperial duties.
  • 皇運 - Mandate of Heaven, the divine right to rule, often invoked in historical narratives.
  • 不覺歲云暮 - Poetic expression meaning 'unconsciously, the year has come to an end,' emphasizing the passage of time.
  • 感極星囘節 - Deeply moved by the Star Return Festival, expressing a profound emotional response to the occasion.
  • 元昶 - Royal or imperial title, though its exact meaning is uncertain.
  • 清平官 - High-ranking official position, often translated as 'minister' or 'counselor.'
  • 法駕 - Imperial chariot or the ceremonial procession of the emperor, symbolizing royal authority.
  • 波羅毘勇猜 - Compound term, possibly referring to a mythical or legendary creature, though its exact meaning is unclear.
  • 河濶氷難合 - Metaphorically suggests difficulties or challenges, meaning 'the river is wide and the ice is hard to join.'
  • 地暖梅先開 - Symbolizes the arrival of spring or a favorable season, meaning 'the ground is warm and the plum blossoms bloom early.'
  • 俚柔 - Refers to the common people or the general populace.
  • 獻賝 - To present tribute or offer gifts, often in a ceremonial context.
  • 弄防 - Term that is unclear in meaning, possibly referring to a place or a people.
  • 千載侍防台 - To serve at the shelter platform for a thousand years, expressing a wish for long-lasting service or loyalty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南诏 - Historical kingdom located in what is now Yunnan, China, during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 骠信 - Ruler of the Nanzhao Kingdom, often referred to as the 'King of Nanzhao' or 'Emperor of Nanzhao.'
  • 震旦 - Ancient name for China, used in historical and literary contexts.
  • 皇運 - Mandate of Heaven, a concept in Chinese political thought that justified the rule of the emperor.
  • 元昶 - Royal or imperial title, though its exact meaning is uncertain.
  • 清平官 - High-ranking official position in the Nanzhao Kingdom, often translated as 'minister' or 'counselor.'
  • 法駕 - Imperial chariot or the ceremonial procession of the emperor, symbolizing royal authority.
  • 波羅毘勇猜 - Compound term, possibly referring to a mythical or legendary creature, though its exact meaning is unclear.
  • 俚柔 - Refers to the common people or the general populace.
  • 獻賝 - To present tribute or offer gifts, often in a ceremonial context.
  • 弄防 - Term that is unclear in meaning, possibly referring to a place or a people.
  • 千載侍防台 - To serve at the shelter platform for a thousand years, expressing a wish for long-lasting service or loyalty.

Proper Nouns

  • 南诏 - Nanzhao Kingdom.
  • 骠信 - Ruler of the Nanzhao Kingdom.
  • 震旦 - Ancient name for China.
  • 清平官 - High-ranking official position in the Nanzhao Kingdom.
  • 赵叔达 - Minister Zhao Shuda.
  • 波罗毘勇猜 - Compound term, possibly referring to a mythical or legendary creature.
  • 俚柔 - Common people or the general populace.
  • 弄防 - Term that is unclear in meaning, possibly referring to a place or a people.
  • 千载侍防台 - To serve at the shelter platform for a thousand years.

[5658]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 獠妇生子便起其夫卧牀褥饮食皆如乳妇稍不卫防其孕妇疾皆生焉 - Lao woman gives birth and husband takes over care, lying on the bed and eating and drinking like a nursing mother. If not properly guarded against, diseases of pregnant women would all arise.
  • 炊防樵苏自若 - Cooking, guarding, and gathering firewood as usual.
  • 越俗其妻或诞子经三日便澡身于溪河返具糜以饷壻壻拥衾抱雏坐于寝榻称为产翁其颠倒有如此 - Yue custom of wife giving birth, bathing in the river three days later, and husband taking over care of the baby.
  • 南人率不信释氏虽有一二佛寺吏课其为僧以督责释之土田及施财间有一二僧喜拥妇食肉但居其家不能少解佛事土人以女配僧呼之为师郎或有疾以纸为圆钱置佛像旁或请僧设食翌日宰羊豕以噉之目曰除斋 - Southerners generally do not believe in Buddhism, but officials force them to become monks to oversee their land and donations. Monks enjoy the company of women and eat meat, but live in their homes without much understanding of Buddhist practices.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 獠 - Lao ethnic group in ancient China.
  • 越 - Yue ethnic group in ancient China.
  • 释氏 - Buddhism.
  • 南楚新闻 - Historical record or news source from southern China.
  • 投杂録 - Collection of miscellaneous records or notes.

Proper Nouns

  • 獠 - The Lao ethnic group.
  • 越 - The Yue ethnic group.
  • 释氏 - Buddhism.
  • 南楚新闻 - A historical record or news source from southern China.
  • 投杂録 - A collection of miscellaneous records or notes.

[5659]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 缁流 - Refers to Buddhist monks or scholars; in context, 'small southern prefectures often lack Buddhist monks or scholars.'
  • 宣德音 - 'Proclaiming imperial edicts'; in context, 'when proclaiming imperial edicts, one must pretend to be a monk or Taoist to accompany the ceremony.'
  • 赦文 - 'Imperial pardon edict'; in context, 'when the imperial pardon edict reached the subordinate prefecture of Yazhou, which had never had a Buddhist temple, and when it was time to appoint someone to read the edict, a fake monk refused to take his place.'
  • 相思药 - 'Aphrodisiac'; in context, 'a term for aphrodisiacs, often associated with folklore and traditional medicine.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐昭宗 - Emperor Zhaozong of Tang, the last emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 栁韬 - A historical figure who served as an envoy for declaring edicts.
  • 崖州 - A prefecture in southern China, known for its remote location.
  • 文宣王 - Refers to Confucius, who was posthumously honored as the Duke of Wenxuan.
  • 番禺 - A district in Guangzhou, historically significant for its trade and cultural activities.
  • 端午 - The Dragon Boat Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence describes the lack of Buddhist monks in small southern prefectures and the need to pretend to be a monk or Taoist when proclaiming imperial edicts.
  • This sentence explains the situation in Yazhou, where a fake monk refused to take his place during the reading of an imperial pardon edict.
  • This sentence describes the frequent reports of lost vegetable gardens and the request for the prefectural governor to issue a decree to retrieve them.
  • This sentence explains the local people's explanation to the northern visitor about the unique conditions of the shallow waters.
  • This sentence describes the encounter with the old woman selling aphrodisiacs during the Dragon Boat Festival.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐昭宗 - Emperor Zhaozong of Tang.
  • 栁韬 - A historical figure.
  • 崖州 - A prefecture in southern China.
  • 文宣王 - Confucius.
  • 番禺 - A district in Guangzhou.
  • 端午 - The Dragon Boat Festival.

[5660]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 躬庖厨勤刀机 - Diligently working in the kitchen and with cooking tools.
  • 善醯醢葅鲊者得为大好女矣 - Those who are good at making pickles and preserved fish are considered excellent women.
  • 斯岂遐裔之天性欤 - Is this the natural disposition of people from remote regions?
  • 我女裁袍补袄即灼然不防若修治水蛇黄鳝即一条必胜一条矣 - My daughter can mend coats and vests; she is clearly not afraid to handle water snakes and yellow eels, and she can outdo one by one.
  • 凡有筵防斯为上味 - Frogs are considered the best dish at any banquet.
  • 先于釡中置氷次下小芋烹之 - First place ice in the pot, then add small yams and cook them.
  • 一一捧芋而熟如此呼为抱芋羮 - Cook the yams one by one, and this dish is called 'Embracing Yam Soup.'
  • 又或先于汤内安笋笴后投蛙及进于筵上皆执笋笴瞪目张口 - Sometimes, bamboo shoots are placed in the soup first, then the frogs are added, and when served, they are held with bamboo shoots, with wide-open eyes and mouths.
  • 座客有戏之曰卖灯心者 - A guest jokes, saying, 'Selling lamp wicks.'
  • 又云介皮者最佳掷于沸汤即跃出其皮自脱矣 - Another says that the best are those with a protective layer, which will jump out of the boiling water and peel off by itself.
  • 皮既脱乃可以修馔时有一叟闻兹语大以为不可云切不得除此锦袄子其味絶珎闻之者莫不大笑 - Once the skin is removed, it can be prepared. An old man heard this and strongly disagreed, saying, 'Do not cut off this beautiful coat; its flavor is extremely precious.' Those who heard this all laughed.
  • 岭南獠民好为啷 - The Lao people of Lingnan enjoy eating a certain dish.
  • 即防胎未瞬通身赤蠕者饲之以钉之筵上嗫嗫而行以筯挟取咬之啷啷作声故曰啷 - It is a dish made from a creature that has not yet opened its eyes, is red all over, and wriggles. It is fed with a certain food, placed on the table, and eaten with chopsticks, making a 'ling-ling' sound when bitten, hence the name 'Ling-ling.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 岭南 - Refers to the Lingnan region, a historical and geographical area in southern China.
  • 百越 - Refers to the Baiyue people, a group of ancient ethnic groups in southern China.
  • 筵防 - Refers to a banquet or feast.
  • 釡 - Refers to a cooking pot.
  • 氷 - Refers to ice.
  • 小芋 - Refers to small yams.
  • 鱼眼 - Refers to the appearance of boiling water.
  • 抱芋羮 - A specific dish made from yams.
  • 笋笴 - Refers to bamboo shoots.
  • 灯心 - Refers to lamp wicks.
  • 介皮 - Refers to a protective layer.
  • 锦袄子 - Refers to a beautiful coat.
  • 獠民 - Refers to the Lao people, an ethnic group in southern China.
  • 防胎 - Refers to a creature that has not yet opened its eyes.
  • 赤蠕 - Refers to a red, wriggling creature.
  •  - Refers to a specific food.
  • 筯 - Refers to chopsticks.
  • 啷啷 - Describes the sound made when the food is bitten.

Proper Nouns

  • 岭南 - The Lingnan region.
  • 百越 - The Baiyue people.
  • 筵防 - A banquet or feast.
  • 釡 - A cooking pot.
  • 氷 - Ice.
  • 小芋 - Small yams.
  • 鱼眼 - The appearance of boiling water.
  • 抱芋羮 - A specific dish made from yams.
  • 笋笴 - Bamboo shoots.
  • 灯心 - Lamp wicks.
  • 介皮 - A protective layer.
  • 锦袄子 - A beautiful coat.
  • 獠民 - The Lao people.
  • 防胎 - A creature that has not yet opened its eyes.
  • 赤蠕 - A red, wriggling creature.
  •  - A specific food.
  • 筯 - Chopsticks.
  • 啷啷 - The sound made when the food is bitten.

[5661]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 南州 - A region or area in the southern part of the country, likely referring to a specific location in ancient China.
  • 王蜀有劉者 - Wang Shu has a person named Liu , where '王蜀' refers to the kingdom of Shu, and '劉' is the name of a person.
  • 善于篇章 - Skilled in writing, indicating proficiency in literary composition.
  • 説少年赍益部监军使书案于黔巫之南 - The young man carried a letter from the military governor of Yibei to the south of Qianwu, where '益部' refers to Yibei (a region in Sichuan), '监军使' is the military governor, and '黔巫' is a place name.
  • 谓之南州 - Called it South State, indicating the name of the region.
  • 州多山险路细不通乗骑 - The state has many mountainous and dangerous roads that are too narrow for riding horses, describing the terrain.
  • 贵贱皆防杖而行 - Both the rich and the poor walk with canes, indicating the difficulty of travel.
  • 其囊槖悉皆差夫背负 - Their bags and trunks are all carried by hired laborers, describing the method of transporting goods.
  • 夫役不到处便遣县令主簿自荷而行 - Where laborers cannot reach, the county magistrate and clerks carry them themselves, indicating the involvement of local officials in transportation.
  • 将至南州州牧差人致书迓之 - As they approached South State, the governor sent someone to meet them with a letter, describing the reception.
  • 至则有一二人背笼而前将入笼内掉手而行 - When they arrived, one or two people carried a cage forward, placing Liu  inside and carrying him by hand, describing the method of transport.
  • 在于笼中必与负荷者相背而坐 - In the cage, he had to sit facing away from the person carrying him, describing the position in the cage.
  • 此即彼中车马也 - This is their equivalent of a carriage or horse, comparing the method of transport to modern means.
  • 洎至近州州牧亦坐笼而迓于郊 - When they reached the nearby state, the governor also met them in a cage at the outskirts, describing the reception.
  • 其郡在桑林之间茅屋数间而已 - The county was between mulberry trees with only a few thatched houses, describing the simplicity of the place.
  • 牧守皆华人甚有心义 - The prefect and county magistrate were all Han Chinese and very kind-hearted, describing the officials.
  • 衙各相去十余里亦在林木之下一茅斋大挍三五人逢迎极至于是烹一犊儿 - The government offices were about ten miles apart, also under trees, with a thatched hut and a few officials welcoming him, and they cooked a calf for the meal, describing the reception.
  • 乃先取犊儿结膓中细粪置在盘筵以筯和调在醯中方餐犊肉 - They first took the calf's intestines and fine feces, placed them on the tray, mixed them with vinegar, and then ate the calf meat, describing the unusual meal.
  • 彼人谓细粪为圣虀若无此一味者即不成局筵矣 - They called the fine feces 'Holy Pickles,' and without this ingredient, the banquet would not be complete, describing the importance of the ingredient.
  • 诸味将半然后下麻虫裹蒸 - After half of the dishes were served, they served the wrapped fern shoots, describing the meal.
  • 裹蒸乃取麻蕨蔓上虫如今之刺猱者是也以荷叶裹而蒸之勉强餐之 - The wrapped fern shoots were made from insects on the fern vines, similar to today's spiny caterpillars, wrapped in lotus leaves and steamed, and Liu  ate them勉强, describing the meal.
  • 明日所遗甚多 - The next day, he left behind a lot, indicating the aftermath of the meal.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王蜀 - Refers to the kingdom of Shu, a historical region in Sichuan.
  • 劉 - A historical figure from the kingdom of Shu.
  • 益部 - Refers to Yibei, a region in Sichuan.
  • 监军使 - Refers to the military governor, a high-ranking official.
  • 黔巫 - A place name in ancient China.
  • 南州 - A region in the southern part of the country.
  • 州牧 - Refers to the governor of a state.
  • 县令主簿 - Refers to the county magistrate and clerks, local officials.
  • 桑林 - Refers to mulberry trees, often associated with silk production.
  • 牧守 - Refers to the prefect and county magistrate, local officials.
  • 华人 - Refers to Han Chinese, the dominant ethnic group in ancient China.
  • 玉堂闲话 - Refers to the *Yutang Xianhua*, a historical text.

Proper Nouns

  • 王蜀 - The kingdom of Shu.
  • 劉 - A historical figure from the kingdom of Shu.
  • 益部 - Yibei, a region in Sichuan.
  • 监军使 - The military governor.
  • 黔巫 - A place name in ancient China.
  • 南州 - A region in the southern part of the country.

[5662]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太平广记 - A large collection of stories and anecdotes from Chinese literature, compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature, an imperial compilation project commissioned by the Qing dynasty.
  • 杂传记 - A category of literature that includes miscellaneous biographies and anecdotes.
  • 汧国夫人李娃 - A title and name, indicating a woman of noble status.
  • 倡女 - Literally 'song girl,' referring to a courtesan or performer.
  • 有足称者 - Means 'worthy of praise.'
  • 故监察御史白行简为传述 - Indicates that the story is being told by Bai Xingjian, a former censorial official.
  • 知命之年 - Refers to the age of fifty, traditionally considered a time of wisdom and maturity.
  • 始弱冠矣 - Refers to a young man who is just coming of age, typically around twenty years old.
  • 隽朗有词藻 - Describes someone who is eloquent and well-versed in literature.
  • 深为时辈推伏 - Indicates that he was highly respected by his contemporaries.
  • 此吾家千里驹也 - A metaphor for a talented and promising young man.
  • 应乡赋秀才举 - Refers to a local examination for talented scholars.
  • 将行乃盛其服玩车马之饰 - Describes preparing for a journey with fine clothing and vehicles.
  • 计其京师薪储之费 - Refers to calculating the expenses for living in the capital.
  • 吾观尔之才当一战而霸 - Expresses confidence in the son's ability to succeed.
  • 今备二载之用且丰尔之给将为其志也 - Indicates providing for two years and ensuring he has enough resources to pursue his goals.
  • 居于布政里 - Refers to living in a district called Buzhengli.
  • 还自平康东门入 - Describes returning through the eastern gate of Pingkang.
  • 将访友于西南至鸣珂曲 - Plans to visit friends in the southwest, passing through Mingke Qu.
  • 阖一扉有娃方凭一双鬟青衣立 - A girl standing near a closed door, holding onto two attendants dressed in green.
  • 妖姿要妙绝代未有 - Describes the girl's extraordinary beauty and charm.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卿适在屏间 - 卿 is a respectful term for 'you' or 'you (male)', 适在屏间 means 'just now in the screen (or curtain).'
  • 厥后心常勤念 - 厥后 means 'after that' or 'later on', 心常勤念 means 'constantly thinking about' or 'often remembering.'
  • 虽寝与食未尝或舍 - 虽寝与食 means 'even during sleep and meals', 未尝或舍 means 'never once stopped' or 'not even for a moment.'
  • 娃答曰我心亦如之 - 娃答曰 means 'the girl replied', 我心亦如之 means 'my heart is also like that.'
  • 生曰今之来非直求居而已愿偿平生之志但未知命也若何 - 生曰 means 'the man said', 今之来 means 'coming now', 非直求居而已 means 'not just to seek a place to stay', 愿偿平生之志 means 'to fulfill my lifelong aspirations', 但未知命也若何 means 'but I don't know what fate has in store for me.'
  • 言未终姥至询其故具以告 - 言未终 means 'before he finished speaking', 姥至 means 'the old lady arrived', 询其故 means 'asked the reason', 具以告 means 'told her everything.'
  • 姥笑曰男女之际大欲存焉情茍相得虽父母命不能制也 - 姥笑曰 means 'the old lady smiled and said', 男女之际大欲存焉 means 'in the relationship between men and women, there is a great desire', 情茍相得 means 'if their feelings are mutual', 虽父母命不能制也 means 'even if the parents' commands cannot control it.'
  • 女子固陋曷足以荐君子之枕席 - 女子固陋 means 'a woman is inherently limited', 曷足以 means 'how could she be enough to', 荐君子之枕席 means 'to serve as a pillow and bed for a gentleman.'
  • 生遂下阶拜而谢之曰愿以己为厮养 - 生遂下阶 means 'the man then stepped down from the steps', 拜而谢之 means 'bowed and thanked her', 愿以己为厮养 means 'wishes to serve as a servant.'
  • 姥遂目之为郎饮酣而散及旦尽徙其囊槖因家于李之第 - 姥遂目之为郎 means 'the old lady then regarded him as her son-in-law', 饮酣而散 means 'drank to their hearts' content and then dispersed', 及旦尽徙其囊槖 means 'by dawn, they had moved all his belongings', 因家于李之第 means 'and settled in Li's mansion.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 屏间 - Refers to a screen or curtain, often used in traditional Chinese settings.
  • 竹林神 - Refers to the gods of the Bamboo Forest, which were often associated with fertility and good fortune in Chinese folklore.
  • 倡优侪 - Refers to entertainers and jesters, often considered lower-class individuals in traditional Chinese society.
  • 厮养 - Refers to a servant or caretaker, often in a household setting.
  • 竹林 - The Bamboo Forest is a symbol of seclusion and tranquility, often associated with scholars and recluses in Chinese literature.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 卿适在屏间厥后心常勤念虽寝与食未尝或舍 - This sentence is a bit elliptical. It can be read as 'You were just now behind the screen, and from then on, I often thought about you, even during sleep and meals, never once stopping.'
  • 生曰今之来非直求居而已愿偿平生之志但未知命也若何 - This sentence is also elliptical. It can be read as 'The man said, 'I came now not just to seek a place to stay, but to fulfill my lifelong aspirations, but I don't know what fate has in store for me.'
  • 言未终姥至询其故具以告 - This sentence is a bit ambiguous. It can be read as 'Before he finished speaking, the old lady arrived and asked the reason, and he told her everything.'
  • 姥笑曰男女之际大欲存焉情茍相得虽父母命不能制也 - This sentence is a bit elliptical. It can be read as 'The old lady smiled and said, 'In the relationship between men and women, there is a great desire. If their feelings are mutual, even the parents' commands cannot control it.'
  • 女子固陋曷足以荐君子之枕席 - This sentence is a bit elliptical. It can be read as 'A woman is inherently limited; how could she be enough to serve as a pillow and bed for a gentleman?'
  • 生遂下阶拜而谢之曰愿以己为厮养 - This sentence is a bit elliptical. It can be read as 'The man then stepped down from the steps, bowed, and thanked her, saying, 'I wish to serve as a servant.'
  • 姥遂目之为郎饮酣而散及旦尽徙其囊槖因家于李之第 - This sentence is a bit elliptical. It can be read as 'The old lady then regarded him as her son-in-law, and they drank to their hearts' content before dispersing. By dawn, they had moved all his belongings and settled in Li's mansion.'
  • 自是生屏迹戢身不复与亲知相闻日防倡优侪狎戏逰宴囊中尽空乃鬻骏乘及其家僮岁余资财仆马荡然迩来姥意渐怠娃情弥笃他日娃谓生曰与郎相知一年尚无孕嗣常闻竹林神者报应如响将致荐酹求之可乎 - This sentence is quite long and elliptical. It can be read as 'From then on, the man secluded himself, no longer communicating with his relatives and friends. He guarded against the company of entertainers and jesters, and his purse was empty, so he sold his fine horse and servants. After a year, his wealth and servants were gone. Recently, the old lady's interest waned, but the girl's affection grew stronger. One day, the girl said to the man, 'We have known each other for a year but still no offspring. I often hear that the gods of the Bamboo Forest respond quickly to prayers. Shall we offer a sacrifice and pray for it?'

  • 初二肆之佣凶器者互争胜负其东肆车轝皆竒丽殆不敌唯哀挽劣焉 - The workers in the two shops competed to see who was better. The eastern shop's carriages and sedan chairs were so beautiful that they were almost unbeatable, but only the mournful songs were inferior.
  • 其东肆长知生妙绝乃醵钱二万索顾焉其党耆旧共较其所能者阴教生新声而相赞和 - The head of the eastern shop knew he was exceptionally skilled, collected two thousand coins to hire him. The elders of the shop competed to see who was better at what they could do, secretly taught him new songs, and praised and harmonized with him.
  • 累旬人莫知之其二肆长相谓曰我欲各阅所佣之器于天门街以较优劣不胜者罚直五万以备酒馔之用可乎 - For several weeks, no one knew about it. The heads of the two shops said to each other, 'I want to display the items we make on Tianmen Street to compare their quality. The loser will pay five thousand coins to prepare for a feast. Is that okay?'
  • 二肆许诺乃邀立符契署以保证然后阅之士女大和会聚至数万于是里胥告于贼曹贼曹闻于京尹四方之士尽赴趋焉巷无 - Both shops agreed, then invited witnesses to sign a contract to ensure the agreement, then they displayed their items. Men and women gathered, up to tens of thousands. Then the village head reported to the local police, who reported to the city magistrate. People from all directions rushed to attend. The streets were empty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 凶肆 - Refers to a place where funeral items are sold or prepared. In ancient China, funeral shops were common and played a significant role in the funeral industry.
  • 繐帷 - Refers to the white mourning cloth used in funerals. The use of white cloth was a symbol of mourning in ancient Chinese culture.
  • 长安 - Refers to Chang'an, the capital city of the Han and Tang dynasties, known for its cultural and economic importance.
  • 天门街 - Refers to Tianmen Street, a bustling street in Chang'an known for its commercial and social activities.
  • 贼曹 - Refers to the local police or law enforcement officials in ancient China.
  • 京尹 - Refers to the city magistrate or the highest administrative official in the city.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 然流涕 - Classical expression meaning 'to weep profusely' or 'to shed tears uncontrollably.'
  • 生父驚而詰之 - The father was startled and questioned him; '詰' means to interrogate or question sternly.
  • 歌者之貌酷似郎之亡子 - The singer's appearance is very similar to my deceased son; '酷似' means 'very similar' or 'strikingly alike.'
  • 吾子以多財為盜所害 - My son was killed by a thief because of his wealth; '以多財為盜所害' implies that the son's wealth led to his death.
  • 奚至是耶 - 'Why is this so?' or 'Why is it like this?' where '奚' is an interrogative particle.
  • 言訖亦泣 - After saying this, he also wept; '訖' means 'after finishing' or 'after saying.'
  • 防問 - Refers to a formal inquiry or investigation.
  • 同黨 - Refers to fellow members of a group or community.
  • 向歌者誰若斯之妙欤 - Who was the singer who was so wonderful? '若斯之妙' means 'so wonderful' or 'so excellent.'
  • 某氏之子 - The son of a certain family; '某氏' is a placeholder for an unknown family name.
  • 囘翔將匿於眾中 - He was about to hide among the crowd; '囘翔' means 'to move about' or 'to hesitate.'
  • 徐往迫而察之 - He slowly approached and examined him; '徐往' means 'to approach slowly' and '迫而察之' means 'to examine closely.'
  • 豎凛然大驚 - The servant was greatly startled; '豎' refers to a servant or attendant.
  • 徐往迫而察之 - He slowly approached and examined him; '徐往' means 'to approach slowly' and '迫而察之' means 'to examine closely.'
  • 生見豎色動 - The young man saw the servant's expression change; '色動' means 'expression changed.'
  • 相持而泣 - They embraced and wept; '相持' means 'to embrace' or 'to hold each other.'
  • 徒行出 - He walked out; '徒行' means 'to walk on foot.'
  • 曲江西杏園東 - West of Qu River and east of Apricot Garden; '曲江' and '杏園' are specific locations in Chang'an (ancient capital of China).
  • 去其衣服 - He removed his clothes; '去' means 'to remove' or 'to take off.'
  • 以馬鞭鞭之 - He beat him with a horsewhip; '鞭' means both 'whip' and 'to beat.'
  • 不勝其苦而毙 - He could not bear the pain and died; '不勝其苦' means 'could not endure the suffering.'
  • 父棄之而去 - The father abandoned him and left; '棄' means 'to abandon' or 'to discard.'
  • 其師命相狎昵者 - His teacher commanded those who were close to him; '狎昵' means 'to be close' or 'to be intimate.'
  • 陰隨之歸 - They secretly followed him back; '陰' means 'secretly' or 'in secret.'
  • 告同黨共加傷歎 - They told their fellow members, who all expressed sorrow; '傷歎' means 'to express sorrow' or 'to lament.'
  • 令二人赍苇席瘗焉 - They ordered two people to carry reed mats and bury him; '赍' means 'to carry' and '瘗' means 'to bury.'
  • 心下微溫 - His heart was slightly warm; '微溫' means 'slightly warm.'
  • 舉之良久 - They lifted him for a long time; '良久' means 'for a long time.'
  • 氣稍通 - His breath began to circulate; '稍通' means 'began to circulate.'
  • 荷而歸 - They carried him back; '荷' means 'to carry' or 'to bear.'
  • 以苇筒灌勺飲 - They used a reed tube to pour small amounts of water; '苇筒' means 'reed tube' and '勺飲' means 'small amounts of water.'
  • 經宿乃活 - After one night, he revived; '經宿' means 'after one night.'
  • 月余手足不能自舉 - After a month, he could not lift his hands and feet; '手足' means 'hands and feet' and '不能自舉' means 'could not lift himself.'
  • 其楚挞之处皆溃烂秽甚 - The places where he was beaten were all溃烂 and very dirty; '楚挞' means 'to beat severely' and '溃烂' means 'to rot' or 'to溃烂.'
  • 同辈患之 - His peers were troubled by it; '患之' means 'to be troubled by it.'
  • 一夕弃于道周行 - One night, he was abandoned on the road; '弃' means 'to abandon' and '道周行' means 'on the road.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 娈万态以中上意 - Pleasing the emperor with myriad forms and appearances.
  • 上益嬖焉 - The emperor favored her even more.
  • 时省风九州泥金五岳骊山雪夜上阳春朝 - Describes the emperor's travels and visits to various places, including a snowy night at Lishan and a spring morning at Shangyang.
  • 与上行同辇止同室宴専席寝専房 - Describes the close relationship between the woman and the emperor, sharing the same carriage, staying in the same room, having a private banquet, and sleeping in a private room.
  • 虽有三夫人九嫔二十七世妇八十一御妻暨后官才人乐府妓女使天子无顾盼意 - Lists various ranks of women in the imperial harem, suggesting that despite their presence, the emperor only had eyes for this woman.
  • 非徒殊艶尤态独能致是葢才知明慧善巧便佞先意希防有不可形容者焉 - Explains that the woman's beauty and charm were not the only reasons for the emperor's favor; her intelligence, wisdom, and ability to anticipate his desires also played a significant role.
  • 叔父昆弟皆列在清贵爵为通侯姊妹封国夫人富埒王室车服邸第与大长公主侔而恩泽势力则又过之 - Describes the woman's family's elevated status, with uncles and brothers holding high-ranking noble titles and sisters being feudal ladies, wealthy enough to rival the royal family.
  • 出入禁门不问京师长吏为之侧目故当时謡咏有云生女勿悲酸生男勿欢喜又曰男不封侯女作妃君看女却为门楣其为人心羡慕如此 - Describes the woman's influence and the public perception of her, with people envious of her status and influence.
  • 天寳末兄国忠盗丞相位愚弄国柄及安禄山引兵向阙以讨杨氏为辞潼闗不守翠华南幸出咸阳道次 - Describes the political turmoil and the fall of the Tang Dynasty, including the An Lushan Rebellion and the emperor's southern retreat.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 上 - Refers to the emperor, specifically Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 三夫人九嫔二十七世妇八十一御妻 - Titles and ranks of women in the imperial harem, reflecting the hierarchical structure of the Tang Dynasty's court.
  • 后官才人乐府妓女 - Lower-ranking women in the imperial harem, including concubines and entertainers.
  • 通侯 - A high-ranking noble title in the Han Dynasty, often used to denote a vassal or a high-ranking official.
  • 封国夫人 - A title given to the wives of feudal lords.
  • 大长公主 - A title given to the eldest princesses of the royal family.
  • 天寳 - The era name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, from 742 to 756 AD.
  • 安禄山 - A military governor who led a rebellion against the Tang Dynasty, known as the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 潼闗 - A strategic pass in Shaanxi province, crucial for defending the capital Chang'an.
  • 翠华南幸 - Refers to the emperor's southern retreat, which was a significant event during the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 咸阳 - An ancient city near modern-day Xi'an, the capital of the Qin Dynasty and later a major city in the Han and Tang Dynasties.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '娈万态以中上意上益嬖焉' is a bit ambiguous and could be read as 'She pleased the emperor with myriad forms and appearances, and the emperor favored her even more.'

  • 梨园弟子玉管发音闻霓裳羽衣一声则天顔不怡左右欷歔 - Emotional response to the music of the Pear Garden musicians, evoking memories of Yang Guifei.
  • 三载一意其念不衰求之梦魂杳沓而不能得 - The emperor's persistent longing for Yang Guifei, even in dreams.

  • 秋七月牵牛织女相见之夕 - Refers to 'the night when the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl meet in the seventh month of autumn,' describing the Chinese Qixi Festival.
  • 秦人风俗夜张锦绣陈饮食花燔香于庭号为乞巧宫掖间尤尚之 - Describes 'the custom of the Qin people to decorate their homes with brocade, arrange food, burn incense, and place flowers on the night of the Qixi Festival, especially in the imperial palace.' This describes a traditional festival practice.
  • 言毕执手各呜咽 - Describes 'after speaking, they held hands and wept,' emphasizing the emotional intensity of the moment.
  • 此独君王知之耳 - Highlights 'only the emperor knows this,' emphasizing the secrecy of their vow.
  • 因自悲曰 - Introduces 'then she sighed and said,' indicating the concubine's reflection.

  • 蓬莱宫 - Refers to the mythical *Penglai Palace*, a celestial abode in Chinese mythology, often associated with immortality and the divine.
  • 长生殿 - Refers to the *Longsheng Palace*, a mythical or celestial place where lovers are believed to reunite.
  • 七月七日 - Refers to the *Qixi Festival*, a traditional Chinese festival celebrating the love between the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl, two star-crossed lovers.
  • 比翼鸟 - Refers to a mythical bird that flies with its mate, symbolizing eternal companionship.
  • 连理枝 - Refers to intertwined branches of trees, symbolizing a deep, unbreakable bond.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 闻道汉家天子使九华帐里梦魂惊 - The phrase is elliptical, with '闻道' (heard that) introducing the dream or vision, and '使' (caused) indicating that the emperor's presence or actions caused the dream.
  • 衣推枕起徘徊 - The sequence of actions is compact, with '衣' (clothes) and '推枕' (pushing aside the pillow) describing the physical actions of waking up.
  • 珠箔银屏迤开 - The phrase is descriptive, with '珠箔银屏' (pearl curtain and silver screen) and '迤开' (slowly opening) creating a vivid image of the setting.
  • 云鬓半偏新睡觉 - The phrase is elliptical, with '云鬓半偏' (hair slightly disheveled) and '新睡觉' (just woke up) describing the woman's appearance after waking.
  • 花冠不整下堂来 - The phrase is descriptive, with '花冠不整' (headdress not properly arranged) and '下堂来' (descending from the hall) indicating the woman's haste or disarray.
  • 风吹仙袂飘飘举 - The phrase is descriptive, with '风吹' (wind blowing) and '仙袂飘飘举' (robes floating gracefully) evoking a sense of ethereal grace.
  • 犹似霓裳羽衣舞 - The phrase is a simile, comparing the woman's movement to the celestial dance described in the *Nirvana Sutra*.
  • 玉容寂寞泪阑干 - The phrase is descriptive, with '玉容' (pale face) and '泪阑干' (tears streaming down) creating a poignant image of sorrow.
  • 梨花一枝春带雨 - The phrase is a metaphor, comparing the woman's tears to raindrops on a branch of pear blossoms.
  • 含情凝睇谢君王 - The phrase is descriptive, with '含情' (filled with emotion) and '凝睇' (gazing intently) describing the woman's gaze as she looks at the king.
  • 一别音容两渺茫 - The phrase is descriptive, with '一别' (after a separation) and '音容两渺茫' (voice and presence fading) indicating the loss of the king's presence.
  • 昭阳殿恩爱绝 - The phrase is descriptive, with '昭阳殿' (palace) and '恩爱绝' (love and intimacy lost) indicating the loss of love in the emperor's palace.
  • 蓬莱宫中日月长 - The phrase is descriptive, with '蓬莱宫' (celestial palace) and '日月长' (time passing) suggesting a sense of eternity or distance.
  • 回头下望人寰处 - The phrase is descriptive, with '回头' (looking back) and '下望人寰处' (looking down toward the mortal world) indicating the act of looking down from a high place.
  • 不见长安见尘雾 - The phrase is descriptive, with '不见长安' (unable to see Chang'an) and '见尘雾' (seeing only mist) symbolizing separation or obscurity.
  • 空将旧物表深情 - The phrase is descriptive, with '空将' (sending) and '旧物' (old objects) indicating the act of sending gifts to express deep feelings.
  • 钿合金钗寄将去 - Refers to the exchange of jewelry, specifically hairpins and bracelets, as a symbol of their bond.
  • 钗留一股合一扇 - Describes the division of the hairpins, with one part kept and the other sent away, symbolizing a shared memory.
  • 钗劈黄金合分钿 - Indicates the breaking of the jewelry, with the gold and silver parts separated, symbolizing the severing of their connection.
  • 但令心似金钿坚 - Expresses the wish for their hearts to remain as unbreakable as the jewelry, despite physical separation.
  • 天上人间会相见 - Expresses the hope of eventual reunion, whether in heaven or on earth.
  • 临别殷勤重寄词 - Describes the act of earnestly sending a message or poem as a farewell.
  • 词中有誓两心知 - Indicates that the words contain a promise or vow known only to the two of them.
  • 七月七日长生殿 - Refers to the *Qixi Festival* (Double Seventh Festival), a day when lovers are believed to reunite in the heavens.
  • 夜半无人私语时 - Describes a moment of intimate conversation in the middle of the night, when no one else is around.
  • 在天愿为比翼鸟 - Expresses the wish to be like birds that fly together, symbolizing a desire for eternal companionship.
  • 在地愿为连理枝 - Expresses the wish to be like intertwined branches, symbolizing a deep, unbreakable bond.
  • 天长地久有时尽 - Indicates that even the longest-lasting things, such as heaven and earth, will eventually come to an end.
  • 此恨绵绵无绝期 - Expresses the enduring nature of the sorrow or regret, suggesting it will never truly end.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 軍王遂中宅仙客曰 - A complex phrase indicating that 'Wang Suozhong (遂中), the military official, in his residence, said to Xianke...' This is a classical way of introducing a character’s speech, with the speaker’s status and location mentioned before the dialogue.
  • 無雙固無見期 - A rhetorical expression meaning 'Wushuang (無雙) certainly has no fixed time to be seen.' It implies that Wushuang is not expected to be seen or is expected to be unattainable.
  • 得見采苹死亦足矣 - A dramatic expression meaning 'If I can just see Cai Ping (采苹) once, even if she dies, it would be enough.' This reflects deep emotional longing and sacrifice.
  • 刺谒以从侄礼见 - '刺谒' refers to a formal introduction or recommendation letter, and '从侄礼' is the ceremonial protocol for a cousin or younger relative. Together, it means 'submitted a recommendation letter and was received according to the rites of a cousin.'
  • 具道本末 - A classical phrase meaning 'explained the full details' or 'gave a complete account.'
  • 納厚價以赎采苹 - 'To pay a high price to redeem Cai Ping.' This reflects the high value placed on Cai Ping’s life or freedom.
  • 深见相知 - 'Deeply understood and showed affection.' Indicates a strong emotional or intellectual connection.
  • 税屋与鸿苹居 - 'Taxed his house to live with Hongping (鸿苹).' This is likely a misunderstanding or mistranslation; more likely, it means 'rented a house to live with Hongping.'
  • 塞鸿每言 - 'Saihong (塞鸿) often said.' This is a direct speech marker, indicating that Saihong frequently expressed her thoughts.
  • 郎君年渐长合求官职 - 'Since the young master is growing older, he should seek an official position.' This reflects social expectations for a young man to pursue a career.
  • 悒悒不乐何以遣时 - 'Sorrowful and unhappy, how to pass the time?' This is a rhetorical expression of melancholy and the desire for distraction.
  • 以情恳告 - 'To plead with sincerity.' Indicates earnest and emotional appeal.
  • 荐见仙客于京兆尹李齐运 - 'Recommended Xianke to Li Qiyun, the Governor of Jingzhao.' This is a formal bureaucratic act.
  • 前衔为富平县尹知长乐驿 - 'Previously held the title of County Magistrate of Fuping and served as the administrator of Changle Post.' This is a formal description of an official’s previous position.
  • 中使押领内家三十人往园陵以备洒扫 - 'An imperial messenger led thirty palace ladies to the imperial tombs to prepare for cleaning.' This is a description of an imperial ritual or duty.
  • 汝为我一窥可乎 - 'Can you take a look for me?' A request for someone else to do a task on one’s behalf.
  • 宫嫔数千岂便及无双 - 'With thousands of palace concubines, how can you be sure to find Wushuang?' A rhetorical question expressing doubt.
  • 汝但去人事亦未可定 - 'Just go and see, but human affairs are uncertain.' This is a pragmatic statement about the unpredictability of human events.
  • 烹茗于外 - 'To prepare tea outside the hall.' This is a description of a servant’s duty.
  • 坚守茗具无暂舍去 - 'Stay and guard the tea utensils, do not leave for a moment.' This is an instruction to remain in position.
  • 忽有所覩即疾报 - 'If you suddenly see something, report it immediately.' This is a direct instruction to observe and report.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 軍王遂中 - Likely a military official or commander. '軍' indicates a military title, and '遂中' could be a name or title.
  • 仙客 - A name, possibly of a commoner or scholar, given the context of his emotional plea and desire for official status.
  • 塞鸿 - A name, likely a servant or companion of Xianke. '塞鸿' may be a name meaning 'blocking the goose' or 'a goose in the pass,' possibly symbolic.
  • 京兆尹李齐运 - Li Qiyun, the Governor of Jingzhao (modern-day Xi’an). This is a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty bureaucracy.
  • 富平县尹 - The County Magistrate of Fuping, a title of local administration.

  • 内家 - A term for palace ladies or concubines, often used in the context of the imperial harem.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 而鼔其声甚怨 - This phrase can be read as 'and the drum sound was very mournful' or 'and the drum was beaten with a very mournful sound.'
  • 邂逅到此，诸娘子又偶相访 - This sequence can be read as 'happened to arrive here, and the ladies happened to visit' or 'chanced upon this place, and the ladies met by chance.'
  • 今无以尽平生欢 - This phrase can be read as 'now there is no way to fully enjoy the joy of a lifetime' or 'now there is no way to fully experience the joy of a lifetime.'
  • 盍各赋诗言志 - This phrase can be read as 'why not each write a poem to express one's aspirations' or 'let us each write a poem to express our aspirations.'
  • 遂各授与牋笔 - This phrase can be read as 'then each was given writing materials' or 'thereupon each was given writing materials.'
  • 逡巡诗成 - This phrase can be read as 'in a short while, the poems were completed' or 'soon, the poems were completed.'
  • 月寝花宫得奉君 - This phrase can be read as 'in the luxurious living quarters, to serve Your Majesty' or 'in the moonlit and flowered palace, to serve Your Majesty.'
  • 至今犹媿管夫人 - This phrase can be read as 'still feel ashamed of Guan夫人' or 'still feel ashamed like Guan夫人.'
  • 汉家旧是笙歌处 - This phrase can be read as 'the Han dynasty used to be a place of music and singing' or 'the Han dynasty was once a place of music and singing.'
  • 烟草几经秋复春 - This phrase can be read as 'grass and trees have gone through many autumns and springs' or 'grass and trees have experienced many cycles of autumn and spring.'

  • 自从无力休行道且作头陀不系身 - '自从无力休行道' means 'since he has no strength to continue on the path,' and '且作头陀不系身' suggests he has become a wandering monk who is not bound by worldly ties.
  • 又闻满座称好声移时不定去文忽于座内云 - '又闻满座称好声' means 'he heard everyone in the hall praising his work,' and '移时不定去文忽于座内云' indicates that after some time, someone suddenly spoke up from within the hall.
  • 昔王子猷访戴安道于山隂雪夜皎然及门而返遂何必见戴之论 - Refers to the famous story of Wang Rong (王徽之) visiting his friend Dai Kui (戴逵) in the snow at night but returning home without seeing him, famously saying, 'I came to see him, not to see him.' This story is often cited as an example of the value of the journey itself over the destination.
  • 当时皆重逸兴今成君可谓以文防友下视安蒋诩吾 - '当时皆重逸兴' means 'at that time, everyone valued the spirit of leisure and freedom,' and '今成君可谓以文防友下视安蒋诩吾' suggests that the person being addressed is now considered a model of using literature to protect friendships and look down on those like Xu An (徐安) and Jiang Xu (蒋诩).
  • 少年时颇隽气性好鹰鹯曾于此时畋游驰骋吾故林在长安之巽维御宿川之东畤【此处地名茍家觜也】 - '少年时颇隽气' means 'in his youth, he had a sharp and lively temperament,' and '性好鹰鹯曾于此时畋游驰骋' indicates that he enjoyed hunting and riding horses. '吾故林在长安之巽维御宿川之东畤' specifies that his former hunting grounds were in the area of Chang'an, specifically in the direction of Xun Wei (巽维) near the eastern part of Yushu Creek (御宿川). '茍家觜' is the specific location mentioned.
  • 咏雪有献曹州房一篇不觉诗狂所攻辄汚泥高鉴耳因吟诗曰 - '咏雪有献曹州房一篇' means 'he wrote a poem about snow and presented it to Cao Zhou Fang,' and '不觉诗狂所攻辄汚泥高鉴耳' suggests that he was so engrossed in his poetry that he inadvertently defiled a high mirror. '因吟诗曰' means 'he then recited the poem.'
  • 爱此飘飖六出公轻琼洽絮舞长空 - '爱此飘飖六出公' means 'he loves this dancing sixfold snowflakes,' and '轻琼洽絮舞长空' describes the light and graceful movement of the snowflakes in the sky.
  • 当时正逐秦丞相腾踯川原喜北风献诗讫曹州房颇甚赏仆此诗因难云呼雪为公得无检束乎余遂征古人尚有呼竹为君后贤以为名论用以证之曹州房结舌莫知所对然 - '当时正逐秦丞相腾踯川原喜北风' means 'at that time, he was following the example of Qin's chancellor, leaping and dancing in the fields and valleys, enjoying the north wind.' '献诗讫曹州房颇甚赏仆此诗' means 'after presenting the poem, Cao Zhou Fang greatly praised it.' '因难云呼雪为公得无检束乎' suggests that Cao Zhou Fang questioned whether calling snow 'public' (公) was appropriate. '余遂征古人尚有呼竹为君后贤以为名论用以证之' means 'I then cited ancient examples where bamboo was called 'lord' (君), and later scholars considered this a famous discussion.' '曹州房结舌莫知所对然' means 'Cao Zhou Fang was speechless and had no response.'

  • The sentence '自虚曰昨夜已失鞍防今馁冻且甚事有不可率话者大略如斯难于悉述遂策马奔去' describes '自虚' expressing his distress and deciding to ride away.

  • 今乃迫于情礼岂暇缄藏 - Must be read as a single unit, with '今乃' as a temporal particle and the rest as a description of the situation.

  • 君子杀身以成仁徇其毅烈蹈赴汤火旁雪不平乃宝之志也 - Must be read as a single unit, with '君子' as the subject and the rest as a description of the situation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 罗水率维扬之轻锐征八水之鹰扬 - Metaphorical expression describing a powerful and swift military force, likely referring to the Tang army under Li Jing, led by Luo Shixin (罗水) and Wei Shang (维扬之轻锐), attacking the rebel forces (八水之鹰扬).
  • 鞭驱列缺指挥丰隆扇疾风飜暴浪百道俱进六师鼓行一战而成功 - Describes the use of divine powers to control the elements, with '鞭驱列缺' (biān qū Lièquē) meaning to whip and drive the lightning, '指挥丰隆' (zhǐhuī Fēnglóng) means to command the wind god, '扇疾风' (shān jífēng) means to fan the strong wind, '飜暴浪' (fān bàolàng) means to turn the violent waves, and '百道俱进六师鼓行' (bǎidào jùjìn liùshī gǔxíng) means the six armies advance in unison.
  • 朝那一鳞立为虀粉泾城千里坐变汚潴 - Uses a metaphor to describe the destruction of the rebel forces, with '朝那一鳞立为虀粉' (cháo nà yī lín lì wéi jī fěn) meaning that the enemy's remnants are instantly turned to minced meat, and '泾城千里坐变汚潴' (jīng chéng qiānlǐ zuò biàn wū zhū) means that the entire region of Jing becomes polluted and stagnant.
  • 顷者泾阳君与洞庭外祖世为姻戚后以琴瑟不调弃掷少妇遭钱塘之一怒伤生害稼怀山襄陵泾水穷鳞寻毙外祖之牙齿今泾上车轮马迹犹在史传具存固非谬也 - Contains a series of mythological and historical references, including the relationship between the gods of the Jing River (泾阳君) and the Dongting Lake (洞庭外祖), the conflict between them, and the resulting destruction. '琴瑟不调' (qínsè bùtiáo) means a disagreement or discord, '弃掷少妇' (qìzhì shàofù) means to abandon a young wife, '遭钱塘之一怒' (zāo Qiántáng zhī yī nù) refers to the anger of the Qiantang River, '伤生害稼' (shāngshēng hài jià) means to harm life and crops, '怀山襄陵' (huáishān xiānglíng) means to flood mountains and cover fields, and '泾水穷鳞寻毙' (jīng shuǐ qióng lín xún bì) means the fish in the Jing River eventually die.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 再從弟 - A term of kinship meaning 'second cousin' or 'second-degree cousin,' used to denote a relative who shares a common great-grandparent.
  • 三從弟 - A term of kinship meaning 'third cousin' or 'third-degree cousin,' used to denote a relative who shares a common great-great-grandparent.
  • 事從姑 - A phrase meaning 'to care for one's aunt (from the father’s side)' or 'to serve one’s paternal aunt.'
  • 定省 - A classical term referring to the ritual of visiting one’s parents or elders to inquire about their well-being and offer care. Here, it likely refers to the act of visiting one’s aunt as a substitute for parents.
  • 如親焉 - A phrase meaning 'as if one’s own parents' or 'like a parent,' emphasizing the filial care and respect shown to the aunt.
  • 進士 - A degree conferred by the imperial examination system, indicating a high level of scholarly attainment.
  • 共有一驴赴京 - A phrase meaning 'shared one donkey to go to the capital,' indicating economic hardship and shared resources.
  • 投逆旅 - 'To go to an inn' or 'to seek lodging at a roadside inn.'
  • 主人鄙其贫 - 'The innkeeper looked down on their poverty,' showing social bias and class judgment.
  • 辭以客多 - 'Excused himself by saying there were too many guests,' indicating an attempt to refuse service.
  • 喑讷 - A term meaning 'silent' or 'tongue-tied,' often used to describe someone who is speechless or uncommunicative.
  • 進退无所舍 - 'Had nowhere to go in terms of moving forward or retreating,' indicating helplessness and lack of options.
  • 徙倚門旁 - 'To linger by the door,' indicating hesitation and waiting.
  • 商客 - A term for a merchant or trader, often used in the context of long-distance trade.
  • 引之同舎 - 'To invite them to share the same room,' indicating hospitality or assistance.
  • 同舎多喑鳴 - 'The other guests were mostly silent and unresponsive,' suggesting a lack of social interaction or support.
  • 固止之 - 'Strongly prevented them' or 'firmly stopped them from selling the donkey.'
  • 仍资以道粮 - 'Still provided them with provisions for the journey,' showing generosity and support.
  • 擢第 - A term meaning 'to pass the imperial examination and be ranked,' indicating official recognition of scholarly merit.
  • 歴任 - A term meaning 'to have held various positions' or 'served in multiple roles.'
  • 召商客與之抗禮 - 'Summoned the merchant and treated him with equal courtesy,' indicating a reversal of social status.
  • 悚惧逊退 - 'Feared and retreated in humility,' showing the merchant’s reaction to being treated as an equal.
  • 引升堂 - 'To lead him into the inner hall,' a gesture of respect and honor in traditional Chinese society.
  • 出云溪友议 - A reference to the classical text *Yunxi Youyi* (《雲溪友議》), a collection of anecdotes and stories from the Tang dynasty.
  • 高祖 - Refers to Emperor Gaozu of Tang, the founder of the Tang dynasty.
  • 義師數萬 - 'Ten thousand righteous soldiers' or 'a large number of loyal troops,' referring to the military forces of the Tang dynasty.
  • 樵薪貴而布帛賤 - 'Firewood is expensive while cloth is cheap,' a statement about the economic conditions of the time.
  • 采街衢及苑中樹木作樵以易帛 - 'To gather trees from the streets, parks, and palace grounds to make firewood and exchange it for cloth,' indicating a proposed economic strategy.
  • 每匹皆有餘軸之饒 - 'Each bolt of silk had an extra spindle or surplus,' indicating abundance and surplus.
  • 動盈萬段矣 - 'Amounting to tens of thousands of bolts,' indicating the scale of the proposal.
  • 出談賓録 - A reference to the classical text *Tanbin Lu* (《談賓録》), a collection of anecdotes and historical records.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李義琛 - A historical figure from the Tang dynasty, known for his filial piety and scholarly achievements.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 河東大饑 - Means 'great famine in Hedong,' indicating a severe food shortage in the Hedong region.
  • 地十五里生豆 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'a bean sprout grew across fifteen li of land,' indicating an extraordinary phenomenon.
  • 一夕掃而復生 - Means 'it was swept away overnight and grew back again,' describing the miraculous regrowth of the bean sprout.
  • 其實甚圓細 - Means 'its fruit was very round and fine,' describing the quality of the beans.
  • 美人皆頼此活焉 - Means 'beautiful women all relied on this to survive,' indicating the importance of the beans for the local population.
  • 潤州城南隅有樓名萬歳樓 - Means 'There is a tower named Wanwei Tower in the south corner of Runzhou city,' introducing the tower and its location.
  • 俗傳樓上煙出刺史即死 - Means 'It is said that if smoke comes out of the tower, the governor will die,' indicating a local superstition.
  • 不死即貶 - Means 'or else be demoted,' suggesting the consequences of the superstition.
  • 開元已前 - Refers to the period before Emperor Xuanzong's reign, specifically the early Tang Dynasty.
  • 以潤州為店 - Means 'to use Runzhou as a storehouse,' indicating the strategic importance of Runzhou.
  • 甞居此州 - Means 'he once lived in this state,' indicating Dong Wan's past residence.
  • 其時晝日煙出刺史皆憂懼狼狽愁情 - Means 'At that time, if smoke appeared during the day, the governor would be worried and distressed,' describing the reaction to the superstition.
  • 乾元中 - Refers to the middle years of Emperor Suzong's reign, specifically the era known as 'Qiannuan.'
  • 圓可一尺余 - Means 'about one chi in diameter,' describing the size of the smoke.
  • 直上數丈 - Means 'rising several zhang high,' indicating the height of the smoke.
  • 吏密伺之 - Means 'officials secretly watched it,' describing the actions of the officials.
  • 就視其煙乃出于樓角隙中 - Means 'they went to see and found the smoke coming out of a crack in the corner of the tower,' revealing the true cause of the smoke.
  • 更近而視之乃蚊子也 - Means 'they got closer and saw that it was just mosquitoes,' clarifying the nature of the smoke.
  • 楼下有井井中无水黑而且深小虫蠛蠓蛛蝄之色黑而小 - Means 'There is a well below the tower, which is black and deep, and small insects like mites and spiders are black and small,' describing the well and its contents.
  • 每晩晴出自于隙中作团而上 - Means 'Every clear night, they come out of the crack and form a cluster,' describing the behavior of the insects.
  • 遥看烟以手之即蚊蚋耳 - Means 'From afar, it looks like smoke, but when touched, it is just mosquitoes,' clarifying the appearance and nature of the insects.
  • 从此知非刺史亦无虑矣 - Means 'From this, one knows that it is not a sign for the governor to die, so there is no need to worry,' concluding the explanation of the superstition.

[5814]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 顔真卿为抚州刺史 - 顔真卿 (Yan Zhenqing) was a famous calligrapher and statesman during the Tang Dynasty; 为 (wei) means 'to be' or 'to serve as'; 抚州刺史 (Fuzhou Cishi) refers to the position of governor of Fuzhou.
  • 嗜学而居贫 - 嗜学 (shixue) means 'loves learning' or 'is passionate about studying'; 居贫 (ju pin) means 'lives in poverty.'
  • 乡人未之知也 - 乡人 (xiang ren) means 'people of the village' or 'villagers'; 未之知也 (wei zhi zhi ye) is an archaic construction meaning 'did not know him.'
  • 索书求离 - 索书 (suo shu) means 'asked for a divorce'; 求离 (qiu li) means 'sought a separation.'
  • 落托自知求事晩 - 落托 (luotuo) means 'to be destitute' or 'to be poor'; 自知 (zi zhi) means 'to know oneself'; 求事晩 (qiu shi wan) means 'to seek employment late in life.'
  • 蹉跎甘道出身迟 - 蹉跎 (cuotuo) means 'to waste time' or 'to be idle'; 甘道 (gandao) means 'to accept fate'; 出身迟 (chuxiuchen) means 'to come from a humble background late in life.'
  • 金钗任意撩新髪 - 金钗 (jin chai) means 'gold hairpin'; 任意 (ranyi) means 'arbitrarily' or 'at will'; 撩新髪 (liao xin fa) means 'to comb new hair.'
  • 鸾镜从他别画眉 - 鸾镜 (luan jing) means 'a mirror with a phoenix design'; 从他 (congtuo) means 'to let someone else do it'; 别画眉 (bie hua mei) means 'to draw eyebrows differently.'
  • 此去便同行路客 - 此去 (ci qu) means 'from now on'; 同行路客 (tongxing lu ke) means 'to travel the same road as others.'
  • 相逢即是下山时 - 相逢 (xiang feng) means 'to meet'; 即是 (ji shi) means 'is'; 下山时 (xia shan shi) means 'the time to leave the mountain' or 'the time to return to the world.'
  • 真卿判其牍曰 - 判 (pan) means 'to decide' or 'to judge'; 其牍 (qi du) means 'the document'; 曰 (yue) means 'said.'
  • 心虽慕夫高科 - 心 (xin) means 'heart'; 虽 (sui) means 'although'; 慕 (mu) means 'to admire'; 夫 (fu) means 'that'; 高科 (gaokao) means 'high-ranking official.'
  • 身未霑于寸禄 - 身 (shen) means 'body' or 'self'; 未霑 (wei zhan) means 'has not received'; 于 (yu) means 'from'; 寸禄 (cun lu) means 'a small salary.'
  • 愚妻覩其未遇 - 愚妻 (yu qi) means 'foolish wife'; 観 (du) means 'to see'; 其 (qi) means 'his'; 未遇 (wei yu) means 'not yet encountered success.'
  • 曾不少留 - 曾 (zeng) means 'ever'; 不少留 (bushao liu) means 'did not stay at all.'
  • 靡追冀缺之妻 - 靡 (mi) means 'not'; 追 (zhui) means 'to follow'; 冀缺之妻 (ji que zhi qi) refers to a virtuous wife in ancient times.
  • 赞成好事 - 赞成 (zancheng) means 'to support'; 好事 (hao shi) means 'good deeds.'
  • 专学买臣之妇 - 专学 (zhuan xue) means 'to specialize in learning'; 买臣之妇 (mai chen zhi fu) refers to a woman who divorced her husband for a better match.
  • 厌弃良人 - 厌弃 (yan qi) means 'to despise'; 良人 (liang ren) means 'good man' or 'husband.'
  • 汚辱乡闾 - 汚辱 (wuru) means 'to disgrace'; 乡闾 (xiang lv) means 'village' or 'community.'
  • 伤败风教 - 伤败 (shang bai) means 'to harm'; 风教 (feng jiao) means 'customs' or 'morals.'
  • 若无惩诫 - 若 (ruo) means 'if'; 无 (wu) means 'no'; 惩诫 (cheng jie) means 'punishment' or 'admonition.'

  • 一日之内客有数等上至给舍即须法味中至遗即须煮鸡豚或生或鲙 - 这句话列举了不同类型的客人及其所需的饮食。
  • 既而指师老尧佐云如举子此公之徒远相访即胡而已 - 这句话描述了说话者指向尧佐并谈论举子来访的情况。
  • 尧佐矍然逡巡二客告辞而退 - 这句话描述了尧佐的反应及客人的离开。
  • 君牙各赠五缣张汾洒扫内防安置留连月余赠五百缣 - 这句话描述了君牙给客人的礼物及张汾的行动。
  • 汾却至武功尧佐方卧病在馆汾都不相揖 - 这句话描述了汾返回时发现尧佐卧病的情况。
  • 后二年及第又不肯选遂患腰脚疾 - 这句话描述了说话者通过科举考试、拒绝职位及患病的情况。
  • 武元衡镇四川哀其龙钟奏充安抚巡官仍摄广都县令一年而殂 - 这句话描述了武元衡推荐说话者及说话者的去世。
  • 贞元中度支欲取两京道中槐树为薪更栽小树先下符牒华隂华尉张造判牒 - 这句话描述了度支的计划及张造的判断。
  • 召伯所憇尚不翦除先皇旧游岂宜斩伐乃止 - 这句话解释了停止计划的原因。

  • 何不求碑志相救 - Why don't you seek inscriptions on stones to help each other; '碑志' refers to stone inscriptions often used for commemoration.

  • 數人省中縱酒話平生 - Several people in the office drinking and talking about their lives; '省中' refers to the office or government building.

  • 工部员外周愿独云爱宣州观察使怕大虫 - Only Zhou Yuan, the Vice Minister of Works, said he loves the Governor of Xuanzhou and fears tigers; '工部员外' means Vice Minister of Works, '宣州观察使' means Governor of Xuanzhou, and '大虫' means tiger.
  • 自筮仕至秘书监 - From when he started his career to when he became the Secretary of the Imperial Library; '筮仕' means starting a career, and '秘书监' means Secretary of the Imperial Library.
  • 常带使职三入蕃 - Often holding the position of envoy, entering foreign lands three times; '使职' means the position of an envoy, and '蕃' means foreign lands.
  • 殁于赤岭 - Died at the Red Ridge; '赤岭' refers to a specific location.
  • 得莲花漏于庐山 - Obtained the Lotus Water Clock in Mount Lu; '莲花漏' means the Lotus Water Clock, and '庐山' means Mount Lu.
  • 惠远以山中不知更漏 - Huiyuan because in the mountains there is no knowledge of the night watch; '惠远' means Huiyuan, a Buddhist monk, and '更漏' means the night watch or timekeeping device.
  • 铜叶制器状如莲花 - Made a device shaped like a lotus with copper leaves; '铜叶' means copper leaves, and '制器' means to make a device.

  • 每昼夜十二沉为行道之节 - Each day and night sinking twelve times as a signal for the path; '行道之节' means a signal for the path.

  • 善哭每发一声音调哀切 - Good at crying, every time he makes a sound, the tone is sorrowful and poignant; '善哭' means good at crying, and '哀切' means sorrowful and poignant.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '苗躭進士登第闲居洛中有年矣不堪其穷或意为将来通塞可以响卜' is complex and contains multiple clauses. It can be broken down as follows: 'Miao Dan passed the imperial examination,' 'He lived in Luoyang for many years,' 'He could not bear his poverty,' 'He might have intended to predict his future prosperity and adversity by the sound.'
  • The sentence '掃洒防事設幾焚香束帶秉笏端坐以俟一言' describes a ritualistic preparation: 'Sweep and clean to prepare for matters,' 'Set up a table, burn incense, tie up the belt, and hold the tablet,' 'Sit upright to wait for a word.'
  • The sequence '日晏有貨枯魚者至焉連呼之挈魚以入其實無一錢良久方出' describes a series of events: 'Late in the day, a seller of dried fish came there,' 'Called out to him repeatedly,' 'Carried the fish into the house,' 'In fact, he had no money,' 'Finally came out after a long time.'
  • The sequence '遲其出固怒之矣又見或防割其魚因罵曰乞索兒卒餓死耳何滞我之如是邪' describes the gatekeeper's reaction: 'Delayed his coming out,' 'Indeed, was angry with him,' 'Also saw someone cutting his fish,' 'Then scolded and said,' 'Beggar boy,' 'Finally starved to death,' 'Why delay me like this?'
  • The long sentence '初躭甞自外游歸途遇疾甚不堪登升忽見有以辇棺而回者以其價賤即僦而寝息其间至洛东门阍者不知其中有人诘其所由来躭谓其讶已徐答曰衣冠道路得病贫不能致他物相与无怪也吾守此三十年矣未甞见有解语神柩后躭终江州刺史' describes Miao Dan's journey and encounter: 'At first, Miao Dan once encountered a severe illness on his way back from an external journey,' 'Could not bear climbing up,' 'Suddenly saw someone using a palanquin to carry a coffin returning,' 'Because of its cheap price,' 'Then rented it and slept and rested there,' 'Arrived at the eastern gate of Luoyang,' 'The gatekeeper did not know there was someone inside,' 'Questioned how he came,' 'Slowly replied,' 'Properly dressed on the road,' 'Suffered from illness, poor, and could not afford other things,' 'Nothing strange about it,' 'I have guarded this for thirty years,' 'Never seen a speaking spiritual coffin.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 洎韦保衡将欲大拜不能先于恩地将命铎矣 - When Wei Baoheng was about to be appointed chancellor, he could not bypass his connections and appointed Wang Duo instead.
  • 蠙隂知之挈一壶家酒诣铎曰 - Li Biao secretly knew this and took a bottle of home-brewed wine to visit Wang Duo, saying...
  • 公将登庸矣吾恐不可以攀附也愿先事少接左右可乎 - You are about to be appointed chancellor; I fear it will be too late to attach myself to you. May I have a little interaction with you before you leave?
  • 即命酒以饮铎妻李氏疑其堇焉 - Wang Duo immediately ordered the wine to be served. His wife, Li, suspected it was poisoned.
  • 以吾斯酒为鸩乎即命一大爵自引满饮之而去 - Do you think this wine is poisoned? I will drink it myself and leave.
  • 韦保衡欲除裴修为省郎时李璋为右丞 - Wei Baoheng wanted to appoint Pei Shou as a provincial official. At that time, Li Zhang was the Right Chancellor.
  • 韦先遣卢望来申意探其可否 - Wei Baoheng first sent Lu Wang to express his intentions and gauge Li Zhang's response.
  • 相公但除不合先问某卢以时相事权设为礼所沮则伤威重 - You should just appoint him without consulting me. If I were to object, it would undermine your authority.
  • 劝韦勿除 - He advised Wei Baoheng not to appoint him.
  • 唐有士人退朝诣友生见衲衣道人在座不怿而去 - During the Tang dynasty, a scholar, after returning from court, visited a friend and found a monk in his home, which made him unhappy, so he left.
  • 他日谓友生曰公好毳褐夫何也吾不知其言适且觉其臭 - Later, he told his friend, 'You like the monk's robe, why? I don't know what he said, but I just noticed the smell.'
  • 毳褐之外也岂甚铜乳铜乳之臭并肩而立接迹而趋公处其间曾不嫌耻乃讥予与山野有道之士游乎 - It's not just the robe; it's worse than copper slag. The smell of copper slag is so bad that if we stand side by side and walk together, you wouldn't be ashamed to be with me, yet you criticize me for associating with reclusive scholars.
  • 南朝高人以蛙鸣及蒿菜胜鼓吹吾视毳褐愈于今之朱紫远矣 - In the Southern Dynasties, high-ranking officials valued the sound of frogs and wild vegetables over music and drums. I see the monk's robe as far superior to the red and purple of today.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '李蠙与王铎进士同年后俱得路甞恐铎之先相而已在其后也' describes Li Biao's fear of being left behind by Wang Duo.
  • The sentence '迨路岩出镇益失其势铎柔弱易制中官爱焉' describes the loss of Li Biao's influence and Wang Duo's favor with the eunuchs.
  • The sentence '洎韦保衡将欲大拜不能先于恩地将命铎矣' describes Wei Baoheng's decision to appoint Wang Duo.
  • The sentence '蠙隂知之挈一壶家酒诣铎曰公将登庸矣吾恐不可以攀附也愿先事少接左右可乎' describes Li Biao's attempt to ingratiate himself with Wang Duo.
  • The sentence '即命酒以饮铎妻李氏疑其堇焉使玄奴传言于铎曰一身可矣愿为妻儿谋' describes Wang Duo's wife's suspicion and her request for Wang Duo to consider her family.
  • The sentence '以吾斯酒为鸩乎即命一大爵自引满饮之而去' describes Li Biao's demonstration of his trustworthiness.
  • The sentence '韦保衡欲除裴修为省郎时李璋为右丞' describes Wei Baoheng's intention to appoint Pei Shou.
  • The sentence '韦先遣卢望来申意探其可否' describes Wei Baoheng's attempt to gauge Li Zhang's response.
  • The sentence '相公但除不合先问某卢以时相事权设为礼所沮则伤威重' describes Li Zhang's advice to Wei Baoheng.
  • The sentence '劝韦勿除' describes Li Zhang's final advice.
  • The sentence '唐有士人退朝诣友生见衲衣道人在座不怿而去' describes the scholar's reaction to the monk.
  • The sentence '他日谓友生曰公好毳褐夫何也吾不知其言适且觉其臭' describes the scholar's criticism of the monk's robe.
  • The sentence '毳褐之外也岂甚铜乳铜乳之臭并肩而立接迹而趋公处其间曾不嫌耻乃讥予与山野有道之士游乎' describes the scholar's comparison of the monk's robe to copper slag.
  • The sentence '南朝高人以蛙鸣及蒿菜胜鼓吹吾视毳褐愈于今之朱紫远矣' describes the scholar's preference for the monk's robe over the high-ranking officials' attire.

[5853]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不知何許人也 - Standard Classical Chinese phrase meaning 'unknown origin' or 'of unknown background.'
  • 以谀佞事田令孜 - '以' means 'by means of'; '谀佞' is a compound term meaning flattery and sycophancy; '事' means to serve or attend to. So the phrase means 'by flattery and sycophancy, he served Tian Lingzi.'
  • 令孜嬖焉 - '嬖' means to favor or宠爱 (favor), and '焉' is a particle indicating the object of the verb. So 'Tian Lingzi favored him.'
  • 为左军使 - '为' means 'to be' or 'to serve as'; '左军使' is a military title, likely 'Commander of the Left Army.'
  • 奏授朔方节度使 - '奏授' means to recommend and grant an official post by imperial decree; '朔方节度使' is a high-ranking military governor of the Shuofang Circuit.
  • 敕下翼日无疾而死 - '敕下' means the imperial decree was issued; '翼日' means the next day; '无疾而死' means 'died suddenly without illness.'
  • 遐迩仰奉奸豪辈 - '遐迩' means far and near; '仰奉' means to revere and serve; '奸豪辈' refers to corrupt and powerful people.
  • 聚贿千万 - '聚贿' means to accumulate bribes; '千万' is a large number, likely meaning 'tens of thousands' or 'innumerable.'
  • 官至金紫光禄大夫检挍右仆射 - These are official titles: '金紫光禄大夫' is a high-ranking official title with the right to wear purple and gold; '检挍右仆射' is a title of the Ministry of Personnel.
  • 为官所捕 - '为...所' is a passive construction meaning 'was captured by the officials.'
  • 脱身遁于复州 - '脱身' means to escape; '遁' means to flee; '复州' is a place name, modern-day Fuzhou, Hubei.
  • 衣衫百结丐食道途 - '衣衫百结' means tattered clothes; '丐食道途' means to beg for food while traveling.
  • 常为复州后槽健儿 - '后槽' refers to the stable or rear part of a military unit; '健儿' is a term for strong or elite soldiers.
  • 念其蓝缕 - '念' means to think of or consider; '蓝缕' means tattered or ragged clothing.
  • 邀至私舍 - '邀' means to invite; '私舍' is a private house or home.
  • 认以为侄 - '认' means to recognize or adopt; '侄' is nephew; so 'adopted him as his nephew.'
  • 寻获为牧守圉人 - '寻' means soon after; '获' means to be captured or taken; '牧守' is a local official; '圉人' is a stable master or caretaker of horses.
  • 有识者皆目之曰看马李仆射 - '有识者' means people with insight or knowledge; '目之' means to regard or call someone; '看马' is a colloquial term for someone who looks after horses; '李仆射' is the title of Li Dequan, who was a 'Shushou' (a high-ranking official).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李光 - A person's name; likely a fictional or historical figure, not well-documented.
  • 田令孜 - A historical figure, a powerful eunuch during the late Tang Dynasty. He held the position of 'Shizhong' (Chief Eunuch) and wielded significant political power.
  • 僖皇 - Refers to Emperor Xizong of Tang, who fled to Sichuan during the An Lushan Rebellion and later during the warlord conflicts.
  • 陈敬瑄 - A warlord who controlled Chengdu and was a rival of Tian Lingzi.
  • 朔方节度使 - A high-ranking military governor of the Shuofang Circuit, one of the most important military regions in northern China.
  • 金紫光禄大夫 - A high-ranking official title, usually reserved for nobles or high-ranking officials, indicating both rank and privilege.
  • 检挍右仆射 - A title of the Ministry of Personnel, indicating a high-ranking administrative position.
  • 复州 - A historical region in modern-day Hubei Province.
  • 后槽健儿 - Refers to elite soldiers or guards stationed in the rear or stable areas of a military unit.
  • 李安 - A name; likely a fictional or lesser-known historical figure.
  • 牧守圉人 - '牧守' refers to a local magistrate or governor; '圉人' refers to a horse caretaker or stable master.
  • 看马李仆射 - A colloquial or mocking title for Li Dequan, implying he was a former high official reduced to tending horses.

Proper Nouns

  • 李光 - Li Guang (possibly a fictional or lesser-known historical figure).
  • 田令孜 - Tian Lingzi, a powerful eunuch during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 僖皇 - Emperor Xizong of Tang.
  • 陈敬瑄 - Chen Jingxuan, a warlord who controlled Chengdu.
  • 朔方节度使 - Governor of the Shuofang Circuit
